chapter_context.summary: Directs a prophecy of perpetual desolation against Mount Seir (Edom) for their ancient, persistent hatred and bloodthirsty greed for the vacant land of Israel during her calamity.

outline.text: Prophecy of Perpetual Desolation Against Mount Seir (Edom)
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Prophecy of Perpetual Desolation Against Mount Seir (Edom) (35:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Rebuking Edom's Greedy Ambition to Seize Israel's Vacated Lands
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking Edom's Greedy Ambition to Seize Israel's Vacated Lands (35:10-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies of Hope and Restoration
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies of hope and restoration, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 35, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
